Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are constructing a DECLARE model for the given scenario We have a set of activities and we need to define constraints for them

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Since the scenario is about a product design and launch process we can assume some typical declarative constraints

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Lets list the activities again with their abbreviations for reference IG Idea Generation DD Design Draft TFC Technical Feasibility Check CE Cost Evaluation PC Prototype Creation LT Laboratory Testing UT User Testing AG Approval Gate MP Marketing Plan FL Final Launch

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to create a DECLARE model with the keys as specified

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Well consider the following constraints some examples but we can add more

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Existence constraints Some activities must occur at least once Example The process must have at least one IG and one FL

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Absence constraints Some activities must not occur or occur 0 times Example We might not want any activity to be absent in a normal process but we can leave it empty or not include

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Exactly one Some activities must occur exactly once Example Maybe IG should occur exactly once per product idea But note the scenario says each product idea so we can assume one idea per process instance

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Init The process must start with a particular activity Example The process should start with IG

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Response constraints If activity A occurs then activity B must eventually occur after A Example After IG we must have DD at some point

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Precedence constraints Activity B can only occur if activity A has occurred before Example FL can only occur after AG has occurred

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Succession constraints Combination of precedence and response If A occurs then B must occur after and B can only occur after A Example IG and DD might have a succession

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Chain constraints Immediate succession Example DD must be immediately followed by TFC

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However note The problem does not specify the exact constraints so we will make reasonable assumptions for a typical process

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets assume the following constraints for this scenario Init The process starts with IG Existence FL must occur at least once Exactly one IG occurs exactly once Response After IG we must have DD Precedence FL can only occur after AG Chain response DD must be immediately followed by TFC

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually lets think Typically after feasibility and cost evaluation we create the prototype But note chain precedence means that the activity PC can only occur if the preceding activity say TFC occurred immediately before However we might have multiple conditions

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternatively we can have For PC we might want it to be preceded by both TFC and CE but not necessarily immediately So we can use precedence constraints for that

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But note The dictionary structure for precedence is a dictionary where the key is a tuple A B and the value is support and confidence

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem statement might be a bit ambiguous but in pm4py for binary constraints the key is a tuple activity1 activity2 So we will structure the dictionary accordingly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Well set support to 1 0 for all constraints as per the problem statement support is 1 0 and confidence to 1 0 as well assuming strict constraints

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Well assume a linear flow for simplicity IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets set init IG existence FL exactly one IG response IG DD precedence AG FL chainresponse DD TFC chainresponse TFC CE chainresponse CE PC chainresponse PC LT chainresponse LT UT chainresponse UT AG chainresponse AG MP chainresponse MP FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The dictionary structure for binary constraints is constraint type activity1 activity2 support 1 0 confidence 1 0 So for init existence exactly one the key is a single activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem says the model is expressed as a dictionary with these keys so we should include all keys but with empty dictionaries for the ones we do not use
